Strode, William, of Devon, cler. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 30 Oct., 1607, aged 15, B.A. 6 Nov., 1610; student of Middle Temple 1612, as son and heir of Sampson, of Dittisham, Devon, gent.; brother of John 1611. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Strode, Arthur, of Devon, cler. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 30 Oct., 1607, aged 18; student of Middle Temple 1608, as son and heir of Sampson, of Dittisham, Devon, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Snellinge, Thomas, of Devon, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 30 Oct., 1607, aged 19, B.A. 6 Nov., 1610, M.A. 21 June, 1615; vicar of Bridgerule, Devon, 1623. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Sommaster, Samuel (Summaster), of Devon, arm. Broadgates Hall, matric. 23 Oct., 1607, aged 16; one of these names knighted 6 Dec., 1616.
Trehane, Richard, of Cornwall, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 30 Oct., 1607, aged 18, B.A. 25 Jan., 1610-11, M.A. 5 July, 1616.
Strode, Adam, of Devon, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 30 Oct., 1607, aged 18; perhaps father of Reginald.
Rowe, Thomas, of Devon, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 23 Oct., 1607, aged 21.

Colmer, Thomas, of Cornwall, 'cler. fil.' Broadgates Hall, matric. 2 Dec., 1608, aged 19; B.A. 4 May, 1612, vicar of Newlyn, Cornwall, 1614, father of Edward 1641 & of Hugh 1642, and grandfather of the next named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Payne, Nicholas, of Kent, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 11 Nov., 1608, aged 18; probably vicar of Bosham, Sussex, 1616, and of Swaffham Prior, St. Ciric, co. Cambridge, 1620. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Hardey, Thomas, of co. Southants, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 2 Dec., 1608, aged 16; bar.at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1616, as son of Richard of Southampton town deceased. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Clarke, Thomas, of Dorset, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 2 Dec., 1608, aged 18; of the Inner Temple 1611, as son of John, of Turnerspuddle, Dorset, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Brewer, John, of Kent, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 11 Nov., 1608, aged 16; of Boxley, Kent, bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1620. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Selbye, Thomas, of Dorset, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 2 Dec., 1608, aged 19, B.A. 2 July, 1612, M.A. 6 July, 1615.
Wills, Thomas, of Cornwall, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 15 April, 1608, aged 18, B.A. 6 Feb., 1611-12.
Rowland, Christopher, of Bucks, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 16 Dec., 1608, aged 23.
Tiffin, Anthony, of Middlesex, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 4 Nov., 1608, aged 18.
Rogers, Mathew, of Bristol, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 1 July, 1608, aged 18.

Izod, Francis, of co. Gloucester, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 3 Nov., 1609, aged 16; of Stanton, co. Gloucester, student of Middle Temple 1614 (as son and heir of Henry, of Toddington, co. Gloucester); died 1653; brother of Henry next-named, and father of Henry 1641. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Littleton, William, of Salop, arm. Broadgates Hall, matric. 8 Dec., 1609, aged 18; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1620, bencher 1638, as of Henley, Salop, gent. (2s. Sir Edward), serjeant-at-law 1640; brother of James 1612. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Stephenson, Owen (Stevenson), of co. Carmarthen. pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 16 Feb., 1609-10, aged 20; rector of Hasguard, co. Pembroke, 1620, and of Llandawke and Pendine, co. Carmarthen, 1626. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Watkins, John, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1609, aged 17; perhaps B.A. from Jesus Coll. 12 July, 1610, M.A. 30 June, 1614; rector of Llanfoist, co. Monmouth, 1613. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Wootton, Edward, of Salop, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 24 March, 1609-10, aged 18, B.A. 20 May, 1613, as Wotton; rector of Wheathill, Somerset, 1629, as Wootton; father of the next. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Stubbes, Henry, of co. Stafford, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 24 March, 1609-10, aged 21, B.A. 14 Feb., 1613-14, M.A. 21 June, 1616; rector of Blore Ray, co. Stafford, 1619. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Marwell, Richard, of Dorset, cler. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 1 Dec., 1609, aged 18; B.A. 17 June, 1613, M.A. 21 June, 1616, rector of Radipole, Dorset, 1617. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Vaughan, William, of co. Brecon, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1609, aged 19; perhaps B.A. from Jesus Coll. 13 Feb., 1609-10, M.A. 6 July, 1613.
Froysell, Edward, of co. Hereford, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 1 Dec., 1609, aged 28.

Martyn, (Sir) Nicholas, of Devon, arm. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 8 March, 1610-11, aged 17; of Oxton, Devon, student of Middle Temple 1613 (as son and heir William, of Exeter, esq.), knighted 15 Feb., 1624-5, sheriff Devon, 1639, and M.P. June, 1646 (l.p.), until secluded in 1648; died 25 March, 1653, aged 60. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Kirkham, Roger (Kyrkham), of Middx., gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 16; student of Middle Temple (as 3rd son of Edward, of St. Mary, parish of Savoy, Middlesex); perhaps M.P. Old Sarum June, 1646 (L.P.), till his death about Dec., 1646. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Challenor, William, of co. Derby, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 16; B.A. 17 June 1613, M.A. 17 June, 1619, one of these names vicar of Brampton alias Braunton, Devon, 1622. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Bayley, Thomas, of Devon, 'cler. fil.' Broadgates Hall, matric. 8 March, 1610-11, aged 16; B.A. from Balliol Coll. 4 Nov., 1614, M.A. 23 March, 1617-18, rector of Langtree, Devon, 1624. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Aylworth, Anthony, of co. Gloucester, arm. Broadgates Hall, matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 17; admitted to the Middle Temple 1611, as 2nd son of Edward, of Aylworth, co. Gloucester, esquire. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Miller, William (Myller), of Devon, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 11 May, 1610, aged 17; B.A. 29 Nov., 1620, M.A. 25 June, 1623, vicar of Christowe 1622, and rector of Willand, Devon, 1634. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Browne, Samuel, of co. Southampton, 'cler. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 16; student of the Middle Temple 1613, as 3rd son of Abraham, of Winchester, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Browne, Jerome, of co. Gloucester, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 23 Nov., 1610, aged 18; B.A. 6 July, 1614, M.A. 22 May, 1617, vicar of Almondsbury, co. Gloucester, 1616. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Rawlins, Thomas, of co. Gloucester, cler. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 15 June, 1610, aged 17; B.A. 17 Dec., 1613, M.A. 6 July, 1622, perhaps rector of Cusop, co. Hereford, 1621. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Porte, John, of co. Stafford, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 21, B.A. 18 June, 1618; student of Inner Temple 1611, as of Ham, co. Stafford. See Foster's Inns of Court Register.
Cooke, John (Coke), 'paup. schol.' from Devon. Broadgates Hall, matric. 15 June, 1610, aged 17; B.A. 30 June, 1617, perhaps vicar of Coleridge, Devon, 1630. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Lee, Alexander, of Devon, cler. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 15 March, 1610-11, aged 17; one of theses names vicar of Puriton, Somerset, 1625. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Stone, Humphrey, of Somerset, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 15 June, 1610, aged 18, B.A. 21 Jan., 1612-13; rector of Slawley, Somerset, 1627. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hore, Thomas, of Devon, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 8 March, 1610-11, aged 17; perhaps vicar of Tywardreth, Cornwall, 1620. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Deane, Anthony, of co. Gloucester, 'paup. schol.' Broadgates Hall, matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 14; son of Edward, of Pinnock, co. Gloucester.
Tickell, George, of Devon, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 8 March, 1610-11, aged 14, B.A. 15 Dec., 1614, M.A. 7 July, 1617.
Brattell, Daniel, of London, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 15 March, 1610-11, aged 18; B.A. 15 Dec., 1614.
Laurey, Edmond, of Devon, cler. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 10 April, 1610, aged 18; B.A. 17 June, 1613.
Horsman, Andrew, of Devon, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 8 March, 1610-11, aged 18; B.A. 24 Oct., 1616.
Cloche, Stephen La, of Isle of Jersey, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 10 June, 1610, aged 23.
Heydon, Robert, of co. Gloucester, arm. Broadgates Hall, matric. 22 June, 1610, aged 19.
Whittington, Andrew, of Somerset, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 23 Nov., 1610, aged 20.
Wickes, Edward, of Somerset, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 18 June, 1610, aged 20.
Pennye, Allan, of Devon, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 8 March, 1610-11, aged 17.
Tincombe, Thomas, of Devon. Broadgates Hall, matric. 12 Oct., 1610, aged 17.

Rous, William, of Cornwall, arm. Broadgates Hall, matric. 14 Feb., 1611-12, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1614 (as son and heir of Ambrose, of Halton, Cornwall, esq.), M.P. Truro 1625; admon. 20 June, 1635; brother of George 1612, and father of Anthony 1633, and Richard 1638. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg. & Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Strode, John, of Devon, cler. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 8 Nov., 1611, aged 16, B.A. 21 June, 1615, M.A. 30 June, 1617; rector of Little Hempston 1629, and of Dittisham, Devon, 1632; brother of William 1607. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Rous, George, of Cornwall, arm. Broadgates Hall, matric. 14 Feb., 1611-12, aged 16; student of Middle Temple 1615, as 2s. Ambrose, of Halton, Cornwall, esq; brother of William 1612. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Manington, Peirce, of Cornwall, arm. Broadgates Hall, matric. 14 Feb., 1611-12, aged 18; of Combeshead, Cornwall; son of Samson.
Wise, Richard (Wyse), of Devon, mercatoris fil.; Broadgates Hall, matric. 8 Nov., 1611, aged 15; B.A. 21 June, 1615.
Dell, Richard, of Cornwall, 'cler. fil.' Broadgates Hall, matric. 11 Oct., 1611, aged 18. See Thomas 1574.
Luscombe, Henry, of Devon, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 14 Feb., 1611-12, aged 20.
Tayller, John, of Devon, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 8 Nov., 1611, aged 16.

Luffingham, Richard, of co. Gloucester, minister, fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 26 Feb., 1612-3, aged 22; B.A. 20 June, 1616, rector of Fretherne, co. Gloucester, 1630; father of Jonathan. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Georges, Thomas, of co. Brecon, cler. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 26 Feb., 1612-13, aged 18; B.A. 20 June, 1616 (as "George"), rector of Llywen Aberlawrenny, co. Brecon, 1617. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Nichols, Thomas, of Salop, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 26 Feb., 1612-13, aged 22; one of these names vicar of Chelmarsh, Salop, 1616.
Whittington, John, of Somerset, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 30 Oct., 1612, aged 20.

Coode, John (Code), of Cornwall, arm. Broadgates Hall, matric. 10 Nov., 1615, aged 17; B.A. 18 June, 1618, M.A. 20 April, 1621; perhaps of Breage, Cornwall (son of John), baptized there 2 Oct., 1597, died in 1672. But see Vivian's Visitations of Cornwall, pp. 95-96, for two of these names.

Selby, William, of Dorset, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 10 Nov., 1615, aged 18, B.A. 17 June, 1619, M.A. 11 May, 1622; perhaps rector of Chipping-Warden, Northants, 1625, until his death, buried there 15 March, 1642-3. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Yard, Thomas (Yeard), of Dorset, cler. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 10 Nov., 1615, aged 17; B.A. 17 June, 1619, M.A. 11 May, 1622; rector of Sutton Waldron, Dorset, 1632, and of Berwick St. John, Wilts, 1635. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Coxeter, Nicholas (Coxiter), of Berks, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 10 Nov., 1615, aged 18; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 23 Oct., 1619, M.A. 26 June, 1622, vicar of Dunstew, Oxon, 1625. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Selly, Abel (Sellie), of Devon, cler. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 10 Nov., 1615, aged 18, B.A. 9 June, 1618, M.A. 7 July, 1621; vicar of Hatherleigh, Devon, 1623; father of John 1641. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Barker, William, of Essex, eq. aur. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1615, aged 18; (?a student of Middle Temple 1614, as son and heir of Anthony, of Sunning, Berks, Kt.) See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Fisher, Edmund (Fysher), of co. Worcester, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 27 Oct., 1615, aged 18; student of Inner Temple 1617, as of Hill, co. Worcester. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Pridham, Richard, of Devon, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 9 June, 1615, aged 21; B.A. 21 June, 1615, M.A. 11 May, 1618; perhaps rector of Haccombe, Devon, 1644. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Martyn, William, of Devon, arm. Broadgates Hall, matric. 10 Nov., 1615, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1617, as 2s. William, of Exeter, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Sarsfielde, William, of Ireland, eq. aur. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. entry 1 Dec., 1615, aged 14; one of these names M.P. Cork city, 1634, and a knight.
Barker, Henry, of Berks, eq. aur. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1615, aged 17; possibly a brother of Anthony 1623, son of Nathaniel 1618.
Stert, Waymond, of Devon, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 10 Nov., 1615, aged 18, B.A. 24 Oct., 1616, M.A. 17 June, 1619.
Donne, George, of Surrey, s.t.d., fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 19 May, 1615, aged 10; called Dunne in Mat. Reg.
Dunford, Thomas, of Dorset, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 27 Oct., 1615, aged 19; B.A. 27 April, 1619.
Martyn, Christopher, of Somerset, arm. Broadgates Hall, matric. 10 Nov., 1615, aged 17.

Fowell, Edmond, of Devon, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 3 May, 1616, aged 18; bar.-at-law, of the Middle Temple, 1625 (as son and heir of John, of Plymouth); M.P. Tavistock circa Aug., 1646 (L.P.), till secluded 1648, Devon 1656-8, Tavistock 1659, Plymouth 1660, till void 9 June. See Foster's Judges and Barristers, & Parliamentary Dictionary.
Tipton, Edmund, of Salop, cler. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 10 May, 1616, aged 18; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 24 April, 1619; vicar of Bishops Frome, co. Hereford, 1625. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Forde, Nicholas, of Devon, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 14 June, 1616, aged 19; student of Lincoln's Inn 1617, as 2nd son of John, of Forde, Devon, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Skinner, Ralph (Skynner), of Devon, cler. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 10 May, 1616, aged 20, B.A. 30 June, 1617; vicar of Aston Rowant, Oxon, 1629. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Merrywether, Antony (Meriwether), of Kent, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 14 June, 1616, aged 18; (s. Edward, of Barfraystone); died in Belgium.
Tanner, Edmund (subscribes "Edmon"), of co. Worcester, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1616, aged 19, B.A. 17 June, 1619.
Sparke, Vincent (Sperke), of Devon, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 22 Nov., 1616, aged 19, B.A. 21 June, 1620, as Sparks.
Chappell, Richard (Chapell), of Devon, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 10 May, 1616, aged 18.
Tawley, Thomas, s. T., of Devon, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 14 June, 1616, aged 20.
Brooke, Thomas, of co. Chester, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 28 June, 1616, aged 18.
Harderet, Martin, of Middlesex, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 10 May, 1616, aged 17.
Wood, George, of Surrey, cler. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 19 April, 1616, aged 19.
Way, John, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 10 May, 1616, aged 15.
Jeffery, Robert, of Devon, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 10 May, 1616, aged 19.
Tovy, Edward, of Wilts, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 10 May, 1616, aged 18.
Calcott, Thomas, of Oxon, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 27 June, 1617, aged 21; B.A. 24 Nov., 1617.

Gumbleden, John, of Southants, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 26 June, 1618, aged 19; student Christ Church, B.A. 16 (or 20) Nov., 1621, M.A. 10 June, 1624, B.D. 2 May, 1632, preacher at Longworth, Berks, chaplain to Robert, earl of Leicester, rector of Llanhary, co. Glamorgan, licenced 4 Dec., 1632, to marry Florence, widow of Edward Gamage of Newcastle, co. Glamorgan, deceased; rector of Llanilid, als. St. Juliet, co. Glamorgan, 1639, and of Coyty, co. Glamorgan, 1645; died Sept. or Oct., 1657. See Ath. iii. 436; & London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Swayne, William, of co. Warwick, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 29 Jan., 1618-19, aged 18, B.A. 7 June, 1621, M.A. 16 April, 1624; one of these names ejected from vicarage of Withybrook, co. Warwick, 1662, for nonconformity; died about 1690. See Calamy, iii. 352.

Nieren, Gasper, of Kent, cler. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 12 March, 1618-19, aged 18; B.A. from Christ Church 6 June, 1622, M.A. 20 June 1625; one of these names vicar of Stoughton Sussex, 1661. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Bright, Robert, of Wilts, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 12 June, 1618, aged 17; B.A. 17 June, 1619, one of these names vicar of Haresfield, co. Gloucester, 1631. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Peard, Hugh, of Bristol, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 29 Jan., 1618-19, aged 19; B.A. 1 Feb., 1619-20; vicar of West Harptree, Somerset, 1622; father of the next. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Stringfellow, Thomas, of co. Warwick, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 29 Jan., 1618-19, aged 19, B.A. 10 May, 1620; rector of Ashow, co. Warwick, 1632. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Martin, Isaac, of co. Stafford, cler. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 12 March, 1618 19, aged 21; rector of Great Bolas, Salop, 1628. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Wade, Samuel, of Somerset, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 29 Jan., 1618-19, aged 19, B.A. 21 June, 1620; rector of Rowberrow, Somerset, 1624. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Tarry, Alexander, of Northants, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 5 June, 1618, aged 19, B.A. 21 June, 1620, M.A. (Pembroke Coll.) 13 June, 1626, as Terry.
Bandinell, James, of Hants, 'cler. fil.' Broadgates Hall, matric. 12 March, 1618-19, aged 17; B.A. from Christ Church 4 Dec., 1622, M.A. 20 June. 1625.
White, George, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 12 June, 1618, aged 17.
Beck, Robert, of Bucks, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 27 Nov., 1618, aged 18.
Floyde, Thomas, of co. Southampton, cler. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 12 Nov., 1619, aged 19; B.A. 10 May, 1620, M.A. 1 Feb., 1622-3, as Lloyd, which see.
Lloyde, Thomas, of Southants, cler. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 12 Nov., 1619, aged 19, as Floyd; B.A. 10 May, 1620, M.A. 1 Feb., 1622-3, as Lloyd.
Jones, Rowland (Johnes), of co. Anglesea, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 18 June, 1619, aged 20; B.A. 7 June, 1621, M.A. 16 April, 1624.
Hurst, Charles, of co. Gloucester, cler fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 18 June, 1619, aged 18; B.A. 8 Dec., 1621.
Higgons, Henry, of Wilts, cler. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 12 Nov., 1619, aged 18; B.A. 21 Feb., 1622-3.
Hu(gh)es, John, of Southants, cler. fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 12 Nov., 1619, aged 15.
Growdon, Henry, of Devon, pleb. Broadgates Hall., matric. 11 June, 1619, aged 18.

Blake, William, of London, arm. fil. nat. max. Broadgates Hall, matric. 15 March, 1620-1, aged 15; student of the Inner Temple 1624, as son of William, of Kensington, Middlesex, esq., master of the Fine Office, who was buried in the Temple Church 27 June, 1645. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Bourne, Elisha, of Northants, 'cler. fil.' Broadgates Hall, matric. 9 March, 1620-1, aged 16, B.A. 22 June, 1624; M.A. from Pembroke Coll. 7 July, 1627, B.D. 14 April, 1638, rector of Misterton, co. Leicester, 1642. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Langton, Robert, of co. Lincoln, equitis fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 5 May, 1620, aged 18, demy Magdalen Coll. 1617-19, B.A. 10 May, 1620; brother of Peregrine 1607, of Roger 1605, and of William 1601. See Bloxam, v. 81.

Ham, John, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 3 Nov., 1620, aged 16; B.A. 29 Nov., 1620, M.A. 18 June, 1623; perhaps rector of Harnhill, co. Gloucester, 1641. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Bourne, Nathaniel, of Northants, 'cler. fil.' Broadgates Hall, matric. 9 March, 1620-1, aged 19; B.A. 12 May, 1623.
Barfoote, John, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 5 (B.A. 10th) May, 1620, aged 20.

Osberne, Gilbert, of Devon, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 7 Dec., 1621, aged 19, B.A. 10 June, 1624; M.A. (from Pembroke Coll.) 17 April, 1627; B.D. from All Souls' Coll. 20 May, 1634, incorporated at Cambridge 1634, canon of Gloucester 1639, until his death 16 Feb., 1656-7; perhaps father of Cary. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Lewis, Meredith, of co. Hereford, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1621, aged 17, B.A. 21 Oct., 1623, M.A. (Pembroke Coll.) 13 June, 1626, vicar of Brecknock St. John 1633, and prebendary in Aberguilly, afterwards Brecon, 1637. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hallet, Thomas, of Dorset, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 19; B.A. 7 Feb., 1623-4; M.A. from Pembroke Coll. 7 July, 1627 (as John); one of these names rector of Chedington, Dorset, 1647. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Randall, Robert, of Dorset, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 23 Nov., 1621, aged 19; B.A. 21 Feb., 1622-3; M.A. from Pembroke Coll. 13 June, 1626, rector of Babington, Somerset, 1627. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Shapley, Bartholomew, of Devon, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 3 May, 1621, aged 18, B.A. 7 June, 1621; M.A. from Pembroke Coll. 23 June, 1625; vicar of Tawton Bishop, Devon, 1631. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hull, Thomas, of Devon, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 19; B.A. 18 July, 1624; perhaps vicar of Godmersham, Kent, 1624, and vicar of Morthoe, Devon, 1625-31. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Williams, Robert, of Dorset, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1621, aged 17; student of Gray's Inn 1618, as son and heir of John, of Marnhull, Dorset, esq. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Stoodley, Peter (Stowdleigh), of Dorset, cler fil. Broadgates Hall, matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 18, B.A. 7 Feb., 1623-4, M.A. (from Pembroke Coll.) 7 July, 1627. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Goldsmith, William, of Bristol (city), pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 18; B.A. 10 June, 1624; perhaps vicar of St. Keverne, Cornwall, 1641. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Smithe, Henry, of Devon, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 22 Feb., 1621-2, aged 18, B.A. 1 March, 1621-2, M.A. 16 April, 1624; vicar of Cornwood, Devon, 1629. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Cooke, Ellis (Coke), of Devon, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 10 Nov., 1621, aged 19; B.A. (sup.) 28 June, 1622, vicar of Dawlish, Devon, 1627. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Badcocke, Nathaniel, of Devon, 'cler. fil.' Broadgates Hall, matric. 19 Oct., 1621, aged 21; B.A. 14 Nov., 1621, M.A. 10 June, 1624, his will proved at Oxford 31 May, 1628.
Jaques, William, of Wilts, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1621, aged 17; B.A. 7 Feb., 1623-4, rector of Littleton, co. Gloucester. See Add. MS. 15,671, pp. 49, 90.
Streete, Nicholas, of Somerset, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 18; B.A. from Pembroke Coll. 21 Feb., 1624-5; M.A. from Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, 1636.
Griffith, William, of co. Gloucester, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 10 Nov., 1621, aged 18 fellow Pembroke Coll. 1624, B.C.L. 17 March, 1626-7 See Gutch, i. 617.
Chaloner, Francis, of Bristol, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 17; B.A. from Pembroke Coll. 21 Feb., 1624-5.
Crouch, James, of Dorset, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 9 Nov., 1621, aged 18; B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 25 Feb., 1624-5.
Dennis, John, of Devon, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 19; B.A. from Pembroke Coll. 21 Feb., 1624-5.
Holmes, Thomas, of co. Stafford, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 18; B.A. 12 May, 1623.
Day, John, of Dorset, 'cler. fil.' Broadgates Hall, matric. 19 Oct., 1621, aged 22; B.A. 14 Nov., 1621.
Cruft, John, of Devon, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 22; B.A. 10 June, 1624.
Dromant, William, of Devon, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 18.
Stronge, Peter, of Wilts, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1621, aged 19.
Gray, Richard, of London, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 7 Dec., 1621, aged 17.
Conny, John, of Salop, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 2 Nov., 1621, aged 18.

Frampton, Robert, of Dorset, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 3 May, 1622, aged 17; B.A. from Pembroke Coll. 15 Feb., 1625-6, M.A. 10 July, 1628, B.D. 10 Oct., 1640, rector of Bryngwyn, co. Monmouth, 1633, and of Shepton Beauchamp, Somerset, 1662. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Dagnall, Stephen, s. Stephen, of Tring Magna, Herts, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 31 Jan., 1622-3, aged 18; B.A. from Pembroke Coll. 9 May, 1626, M.A. 22 Jan., 1628-9.
Rawstorne, Francis, s. Jerem., of London, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 24 March, 1622-3, aged 15; demy Magdalen Coll. 1624-6. See Bloxam, v. 114.
Nicoll, Patrick, s. Philip, of Woolfardisworthy, Devon, sacerd. Broadgates Hall, matric. 14 Feb., 1622-3, aged 23; B.A. 21 Feb., 1622-3.

Browne, (Sir) Thomas, 1s. Thomas, of London, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 5 Dec., 1623, aged 18; fellow of Pembroke Coll., B.A. 31 Jan., 1626-7, M.A. 11 June, 1629 (a knight), D.Med. Padua, incorp. 10 July, 1637, author of 'Religio Medici,' sometime of Shipden Hall, Yorks, settled at Norwich 1636, hon. fellow of College of Physicians 1664, knighted at Norwich 28 Sept., 1671, born 19 Oct., 1605, and died 19 Oct., 1682. See Ath., iv. 56; Munk's Roll, i. 321; Fasti, i. 498; & D.N.B.

White, Nathaniel, s. Thomas, of Finchley, Middx., gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 18 July, 1623, aged 15; perhaps B.A. from Magdalen Hall 6 July, 1622, M.A. 3 July, 1627 (but the entry is scored out); vicar of Market Lavington, Wilts, 1656, until ejected 1662 for nonconformity. See Foster's Index Eccl.; O.H.S. xii. 410; & Calamy, iii. 365.

Sandys, William, 4s. Samuel, of Ombersley, co. Worcester, militis. Broadgates Hall, matric. entry 13 June, 1623, aged 14; of Askham, Notts, M.P. Evesham, April-May, 1640, 1640 (L.P.), till expelled Jan., 1641, (re-elected) 1661, till his death shortly before 29 Oct., 1669, then alderman and J.P. of Worcester; brother of Edwin and Martin.

Parvish, John, s. John, of London, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 4 April, 1623, aged 15; B.A. from Pembroke Coll. 26 Oct., 1626, M.A. 11 June, 1629, B.D. 10 July, 1637, vicar of Holmenext-the-Sea 1633, and rector of Anmer, Norfolk, 1635. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Brittin, Jesper, s. John, of Worcester (city), pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 13 June, 1623, aged 18; B.A. 3 July, 1627, M.A. 15 April, 1630, B.D. 13 March, 1639-40, vicar of White Ladies Aston, 1635. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Huxtable, John, s. Daniel, of Tavistock, Devon, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 23 May, 1623, aged 16; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 22 June, 1626, M.A. 3 June, 1630; rector of Marsh Baldon, Oxon, 1637. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Rumsey, Edward, s. Walter, of Llanover, co. Monmouth, arm. Broadgates Hall, matric. entry 6 Feb., 1623-4, aged 13; B.A. from Pembroke Coll. 26 Oct., 1626; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1635. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
White, Thomas, s. Thomas, of Finchley, Middx., gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 12 Dec., 1623, aged 15, B.C.L. (Pembroke Coll.) 17 Dec., 1632; minister of St. Anne's, Aldersgate. See Fasti, ii. 205.
Denne, Walter, 2s. John, of Canterbury, Kent, arm. Broadgates Hall, matric. 4 April, 1623, aged 17; B.A. from Pembroke Coll. 26 Oct., 1626, M.A. 11 June, 1629, brother of Vincent 1619.
Vaux, Francis, born in Gloucester, s. James, of Merston in parish Hampton-Meysey, Wilts, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 12 Dec., 1623, aged 22; B.Med. (Pembroke Coll.) 28 April, 1626.
Waye, William, 1s. William, of Torrington Magna, Devon, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 5 Dec., 1623, aged 17; student of Inner Temple 1627. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Wilmot, Samuel (Williamot), born in London, s. Edward, of Chaddesden, co. Derby, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 2 May, 1623, aged 18; B.A. (Pembroke Coll.) 21 Feb., 1624-5
James, Juell, 2s. Andrew, of Newport, Isle of Wight, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 13 June, 1623, aged 18; B.A. from Pembroke Coll. 31 Jan., 1626-7; M.A. 11 June, 1629.
Cliffe, William, s. Edward, of East Woodhall, Hants, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 23 May, 1623, aged 20, B.A. 18 June, 1623; M.A. from Pembroke Coll. 28 April, 1626.
Cruse, Nicholas, s. George, of Ashburne, Devon, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 18 July, 1623, aged 18; B.A. from Pembroke Coll, 21 Jan., 1626-7, M.A. 11 June, 1629.
Chamberlayne, William, 2s. Peter, of London, gent. Broadgates Hall, matric. 9 May, 1623, aged 16, brother of Nathaniel and of Robert 1635.
Nixon, Thomas, s. Joseph, of Shefford, Berks, sacerd. Broadgates Hall, matric. 23 May, 1623, aged 16; brother of Joseph 1621.
Bilcliffe, Thomas, s. Thomas, of Norenburie, co. Lincoln, sacerd. Broadgates Hall, matric. 19 March, 1623-4, aged 18.
Shawe, Thomas, s. William, of Minsterworth, co. Gloucester, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 2 May, 1623, aged 21.
Atkinson, Lawrence, s. Lawrence, of London, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 11 July, 1623, aged 18.
Poe, Theophilus, s. Leonard, of London, D.Med. Broadgates Hall, matric. 6 Feb., 1623-4, aged 15.

Hampton, John, 1s. Robert, of Erchfont, Wilts, arm. Broadgates Hall, matric. 2 July, 1624, aged 16, B.A. 7 July, 1624; M.A. from Pembroke Coll. 17 April, 1627, student of Lincoln's Inn 1628. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Farn(h)am, Giles, s. Robert, of Broad Winsor, Dorset, pleb. Broadgates Hall, matric. 2 July, 1624, aged 18.
Jones, Richard, of Bristol city, gent. Broadgates Hall, subs. 10 Nov., 1621, aged 16; perhaps clerk of Magdalen Coll. 1625-8, B.A. 30 June, 1625. See Bloxam, ii. 59.
